title: Jamaican Rice and Peas
intro:
Rice and peas is one of the defining dishes of Jamaican home cooking and appears on tables across the island, especially on Sundays and holidays. Despite the name, the "peas" are actually small red beans simmered until tender and then cooked together with rice in a fragrant coconut broth. Fresh thyme, scallion, garlic, and Scotch bonnet pepper infuse the pot while the rice absorbs the rich, aromatic liquid.

The finished dish is fluffy, lightly creamy from coconut milk, and deeply savory from the beans and herbs. Whole aromatics perfume the rice during cooking and are removed before serving, leaving clean grains and a warm background heat from the Scotch bonnet. It is most often served alongside jerk, stews, or grilled meats, but the dish is satisfying enough to stand on its own as a hearty bowl.

ingredientGroups.title: Beans
items: ½ cup dried small red kidney beans
2 cups water
½ tsp kosher salt

ingredientGroups.title: Coconut rice base
items: ½ cup long-grain rice (preferably jasmine)
½ cup light coconut milk
½ cup water
¼ cup chicken stock

ingredientGroups.title: Aromatics
items: 2 scallions, cut into 2-inch segments
2 cloves garlic, lightly crushed
3 sprigs fresh thyme
½ small Scotch bonnet pepper, left whole

ingredientGroups.title: Finishing
items: ¼ tsp kosher salt
1 tsp fresh thyme leaves, finely chopped

stepGroups.title: Cook the beans
items: Soak the dried kidney beans overnight in plenty of water.
Drain, then place in a saucepan with 2 cups fresh water and ½ tsp kosher salt.
Simmer gently for about 60 to 75 minutes until tender but still intact.
Reserve ¼ cup of the cooking liquid and drain the beans.

stepGroups.title: Rinse the rice
items: Place the rice in a bowl and rinse under cold water until the water runs mostly clear.
Drain well.

stepGroups.title: Build the cooking liquid
items: In a small saucepan combine the coconut milk, water, chicken stock, and the reserved ¼ cup bean cooking liquid.
Bring just to a gentle simmer.

stepGroups.title: Start the rice
items: Add the rinsed rice and cooked beans to the saucepan.
Stir once.

stepGroups.title: Add aromatics
items: Place the scallion segments, crushed garlic cloves, thyme sprigs, and the half Scotch bonnet pepper directly on top of the rice mixture.

stepGroups.title: Cook the rice
items: Bring the pot to a light boil, then reduce to very low heat and cover tightly.
Cook for about 14 minutes until the liquid is absorbed.

stepGroups.title: Rest the rice
items: Turn off the heat and let the pot rest, covered, for 10 minutes.
This finishing rest gives the rice its characteristic fluffy texture.

stepGroups.title: Finish the dish
items: Remove the thyme stems, garlic cloves, scallions, and Scotch bonnet.
Fluff the rice gently with a fork.
Sprinkle with the chopped fresh thyme and the remaining ¼ tsp salt.
